mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator designed to simplify the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It provides custom resource definitions for managing Ray clusters, jobs, and services, making it ideal for machine learning workloads. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the KubeRay Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeRay Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeRay using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the KubeRay repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeRay CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeRay to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayjob.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayservice.yaml

```

> Installs the RayCluster CRD.
> Installs the RayJob CRD.
> Installs the RayService CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeRay using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs KubeRay on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm, specifying the version and namespace.

```bash

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay --create-namespace

```

> Installs KubeRay in the 'kuberay' namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeRay installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the KubeRay components are running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

kubectl get crd

```

> Lists all pods in the 'kuberay' namespace to verify they are running.
> Verifies that the CRDs for KubeRay are installed.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeRay and its associated resources from the cluster. Be aware that this will delete all KubeRay-managed resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay

kubectl delete namespace kuberay

```

> Uninstalls KubeRay from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any critical data. This step involves upgrading KubeRay to the latest version and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> Drains all running pods in the 'kuberay' namespace.
> Upgrades KubeRay to the specified version.
> Verifies that the KubeRay pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kuberay -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "RayCluster" in version "kuberay.io/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the CRDs for KubeRay have not been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the commands from the installation steps.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create pod: Error creating: pods "raycluster-" is forbidden: error looking up service account kuberay/raycluster-service-account: no provider found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the service account required for KubeRay is missing.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service account is created by reapplying the KubeRay Helm chart.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'kuberay' namespace are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to insufficient resources allocated to the pods.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in the KubeRay configuration and adjust them as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command-line tool is not installed or not in the system PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl by following the installation instructions for your operating system.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Ray features and improved stability of existing components.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeRay pods running in the 'kuberay' namespace with a status of 'Running'. Use 'kubectl get pods -n kuberay' to verify.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources and the necessary tools installed.
